subject: English
title: Extended Reading Passages with Questions – My Brother's Great Invention
instructions: Read each passage carefully. Answer the questions using information only from the given paragraph.

passages.paragraph: My brother, Ravi, is always busy in his garage. He calls it his 'Science Lab.' He loves to collect old boxes, broken clocks, and pieces of wire to create something new and exciting.
questions: Who is the main character's brother?
Where does Ravi spend his time?
What does he call the garage?
What three things does he love to collect?
What does he like to create?

passages.paragraph: One Tuesday, Ravi announced that he was working on his greatest invention yet. He wore a pair of oversized goggles and a messy white apron. He looked like a very serious scientist.
questions: On which day did Ravi make an announcement?
What was he working on?
What was he wearing on his eyes?
What was the color of his apron?
Who did he look like?

passages.paragraph: The invention was a robot made from a tin can and a small motor. Ravi called it 'The Snack-Bot.' He hoped it would help him carry snacks from the kitchen to his bedroom.
questions: What was the invention?
What was the robot made from?
What name did Ravi give to the robot?
What was the robot's purpose?
Where did he want the snacks to be carried to?

passages.paragraph: Ravi spent three days building the Snack-Bot. He used lots of sticky tape and blue paint. My sister and I watched him from the door, wondering if the robot would actually move.
questions: How many days did it take to build the robot?
What did he use to hold the parts together?
What color did he paint the robot?
Who watched him from the door?
What were they wondering about?

passages.paragraph: Finally, the big moment arrived. Ravi turned a small red switch on the robot's back. The Snack-Bot began to shake and make a loud buzzing sound like a giant bee.
questions: What did Ravi turn to start the robot?
Where was the switch located?
What color was the switch?
What did the robot start to do?
What did the buzzing sound like?

passages.paragraph: The Snack-Bot moved forward, but it didn't go toward the kitchen. Instead, it started spinning in circles on the rug. Ravi tried to stop it, but the robot was moving very fast.
questions: Did the robot go toward the kitchen?
What did the robot do instead?
Where was the robot spinning?
Did Ravi try to stop it?
How fast was the robot moving?

passages.paragraph: Suddenly, the Snack-Bot bumped into a flower vase. Luckily, the vase did not break, but the robot fell over on its side. Its little wheels kept spinning in the air.
questions: What did the robot bump into?
Did the vase break?
What happened to the robot after it bumped?
What parts of the robot were still spinning?
Was it a lucky situation?

passages.paragraph: Ravi didn't feel sad about the accident. He picked up the robot and looked at the motor. He realized that the wheels were not straight, which made the robot spin.
questions: Did Ravi feel sad?
What did he do after the accident?
What part did he look at?
What was wrong with the wheels?
Why did the robot spin?

passages.paragraph: He went back to his garage to fix the problem. He said that every great inventor fails many times before they succeed. He was determined to make Snack-Bot 2.0 even better.
questions: Where did Ravi go to fix the problem?
What do great inventors do before succeeding?
Was he determined or did he give up?
What was the name of the new version he planned to make?
Did he want the next version to be better?

passages.paragraph: Even though the robot didn't bring us snacks, we were proud of Ravi. He showed us that being an inventor is about thinking and never giving up on your ideas.
questions: Did the robot bring them snacks?
How did the family feel about Ravi?
What is being an inventor about?
Should you give up on your ideas?
Whose ideas were they?
